key_points: Fractals are geometric patterns that repeat themselves at different scales
Self-similarity means a shape looks similar at different magnifications
Sierpinski Triangle and Sierpinski Carpet are examples of fractals
Polyhedra are three-dimensional solids made of flat polygonal faces
Nets are two-dimensional patterns that can be folded to form solids
examples: Projection example: Shadow of a cube on a wall
formulas: Number of triangles in Sierpinski Triangle after n steps → 3^n
Number of squares in Sierpinski Carpet after n steps → 8^n
Number of sides in Koch Snowflake after n steps → 3 × 4^n
exam_tips: Remember the difference between face, edge and vertex
Understand front, top and side projections clearly
Use isometric grids for accurate 3D drawings
